repair_memories_applied.natural_language: When eval fails on ca6k_00045 sigmoid kernel with output mismatch due to manual sigmoid precision, do not implement sigmoid as Muls/Exp/Adds/Reciprocal using a tmpQueue; instead use AscendC::Sigmoid intrinsic on the local tensor.

repair_memories_applied.natural_language: When correctness fails due to output mismatch (e.g., small floating-point differences) in SigmoidCustom operator, do not implement 1/(1+exp(-x)) using Broadcast of 1.0 and Div; instead use AscendC::Reciprocal on the denominator to reduce precision loss.
When sigmoid output mismatch occurs due to numerical precision, do not implement sigmoid as 1/(1+exp(-x)) using Exp, Adds, and Reciprocal; instead use the tanh identity: y = 0.5 * (1 + tanh(x/2)).

When operator output mismatches reference due to manual sigmoid computation using separate tmp queues for Exp, Adds, Reciprocal, Mul, do not implement sigmoid manually; instead use AscendC::Sigmoid and consolidate temporary buffers into a single tmpQueue.

repair_memory_selection.selection_rationale: The current op ca6k_00147 compiled successfully but failed correctness. The chosen memories target output mismatch due to naive sigmoid implementations (e.g., using Muls/Exp/Adds/Reciprocal) which cause precision errors. id=3ba8664f is especially relevant because it comes from a sister op (ca6k_00045) in the same torch#1 category and directly addresses correctness failure from manual sigmoid. ids 0ca36d0e, 48173ce0, and a85e42ea cover similar sigmoid precision issues across sigmoid and swish operators, providing complementary fixes for a likely underlying cause of the correctness failure.
